description: Module-load performance optimization for Hono, Pi SDK, and Pi CLI. Reduce fixed session overhead, IPC/RPC volume, repeated payload transfer, and bootstrap costs while keeping committed benchmark artifacts and progress tracking up to date.

userStories.title: Make the Pi CLI end-to-end benchmark produce a real workload
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need the Pi CLI end-to-end scenario to actually reach model execution so it can be used as a performance signal instead of a broken placeholder.
acceptanceCriteria: The Pi CLI end-to-end benchmark reaches a real workload path and the committed artifact no longer shows `code = 0`, `mockRequests = 0`, and empty stdout/stderr with no network activity.

Running `pnpm --filter secure-exec bench:module-load` produces committed `result.json`, `metrics.prom`, `ipc.ndjson`, `runner.log`, `summary.md`, and `summary.json` artifacts for the Pi CLI end-to-end scenario.

Append before/after benchmark deltas to `scripts/ralph/progress.txt` for the affected scenarios, including warm wall time, bridge calls per iteration, and the dominant IPC counters touched by this story.

If this story reveals another optimization opportunity or unresolved blocker, add it to `docs-internal/todo.md` and append a new `passes: false` follow-up story to `scripts/ralph/prd.json` before marking this story complete.
Relevant targeted tests pass.
Touched packages typecheck.
userStories.notes: Current failing artifact: `packages/secure-exec/benchmarks/results/module-load/pi-cli-end-to-end/result.json` reports `code = 0`, `mockRequests = 0`, and no stdout/stderr. Do not treat the current Pi CLI end-to-end result as performance data until this story lands.

userStories.title: Generate machine-readable benchmark summaries and deltas
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need the module-load benchmark to emit machine-readable method, byte, and timing summaries so each optimization story can track progress without manually re-analyzing raw IPC logs.
acceptanceCriteria: The committed results folder includes generated per-scenario summaries with calls by method, time by method, bytes by frame/method, iteration-level timing, and fixed session overhead fields.

The committed benchmark output includes an explicit before/after comparison against the prior baseline so later Ralph iterations can cite deltas directly from artifacts instead of manually recomputing them.
The benchmark documentation or result format makes it obvious which numbers should be copied into `scripts/ralph/progress.txt` after each story.

Append before/after benchmark deltas to `scripts/ralph/progress.txt` for the affected scenarios, including warm wall time, bridge calls per iteration, and the dominant IPC counters touched by this story.

If this story reveals another optimization opportunity or unresolved blocker, add it to `docs-internal/todo.md` and append a new `passes: false` follow-up story to `scripts/ralph/prd.json` before marking this story complete.
Relevant targeted tests pass.
Touched packages typecheck.
userStories.notes: The current `analysis.md` is a useful one-off report, but the next wave of optimization work needs generated summaries so each iteration can record benchmark movement in `progress.txt` without hand parsing `ipc.ndjson`.

userStories.title: Add raw UDS RTT and per-session phase attribution
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need direct transport and session-phase metrics so we can distinguish socket latency, serialization overhead, and session lifecycle costs from application work.
acceptanceCriteria: The benchmark suite includes a direct UDS ping or equivalent bridge round-trip measurement for at least `1 B`, `1 KB`, and `64 KB` payloads.

The committed metrics break out session create, first execute send, execute receive, and session destroy or equivalent lifecycle phases instead of leaving the current `~109 ms` fixed cost as an unattributed bucket.
The committed results folder captures these new RTT and phase metrics alongside the existing scenario artifacts.

Append before/after benchmark deltas to `scripts/ralph/progress.txt` for the affected scenarios, including warm wall time, bridge calls per iteration, and the dominant IPC counters touched by this story.

If this story reveals another optimization opportunity or unresolved blocker, add it to `docs-internal/todo.md` and append a new `passes: false` follow-up story to `scripts/ralph/prd.json` before marking this story complete.
Relevant targeted tests pass.
Touched packages typecheck.
userStories.notes: Today the benchmark measures full `Execute` and host-side `BridgeCall -> BridgeResponse`, but not raw UDS RTT. The analysis shows a stable `~109 ms` fixed per-session cost that needs phase attribution before we guess at a fix.

userStories.title: Add JSZip and pdf-lib scenarios to the module-load benchmark suite
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need JSZip and pdf-lib benchmark scenarios in the committed module-load performance suite so we can optimize those heavier document/archive workloads over time instead of tuning only Hono and Pi.
acceptanceCriteria: The committed module-load benchmark suite includes startup and end-to-end scenarios for `pdf-lib` and `jszip`, with committed `result.json`, `metrics.prom`, `ipc.ndjson`, `runner.log`, `summary.md`, and `summary.json` coverage for the new scenarios.
The `pdf-lib` benchmark logic is copied or adapted from `/tmp/pdfbench.js`, including a representative document workload rather than a trivial import-only sample.
The `jszip` benchmark uses a representative archive workload so committed numbers cover both module load cost and realistic zip creation or serialization work.
The committed summary and analysis outputs include cold and warm numbers for the new `pdf-lib` and `jszip` scenarios so later stories can track their movement over time.

Append the first committed baseline for the new `pdf-lib` and `jszip` scenarios to `scripts/ralph/progress.txt`, including warm wall time, bridge calls per iteration, and the dominant IPC counters they exercise.

If this story reveals another optimization opportunity or unresolved blocker, add it to `docs-internal/todo.md` and append a new `passes: false` follow-up story to `scripts/ralph/prd.json` before marking this story complete.
Relevant targeted tests pass.
Touched packages typecheck.
userStories.notes: Use `/tmp/pdfbench.js` as the seed for the `pdf-lib` workload. The goal is not just to import the library, but to keep committed startup and end-to-end benchmark numbers for heavier document/archive codepaths so future perf work optimizes them too.

userStories.title: Restore repeated-session compressed JSZip benchmarking
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need the JSZip end-to-end scenario to survive repeated fresh sessions with compression enabled so the benchmark can track realistic compressed archive workloads instead of the current STORE fallback.
acceptanceCriteria: The `jszip-end-to-end` scenario completes all benchmark iterations with a compressed archive path enabled instead of hanging on the third fresh session.
Committed `result.json`, `ipc.ndjson`, `summary.md`, and `summary.json` artifacts show the restored compressed JSZip scenario and preserve the current representative nested archive workload shape.
Focused regression coverage or a reproducer proves the repeated-session hang is fixed and does not regress silently.

Append before/after benchmark deltas to `scripts/ralph/progress.txt` for the affected JSZip scenario, including warm wall time, bridge calls per iteration, and the dominant IPC counters touched by this story.
Relevant targeted tests pass.
Touched packages typecheck.
userStories.notes: US-012 committed a stable STORE-based nested-archive baseline after repeated DEFLATE runs hung on iteration 3 inside `packages/secure-exec/benchmarks/module-load/scenario-runner.ts`.

userStories.title: Eliminate repeated `_loadPolyfill` round-trips within hot workloads
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need the runtime to stop issuing thousands of identical `_loadPolyfill` RPCs during Pi startup so bridge chatter is no longer the dominant cost center.
acceptanceCriteria: Repeated `_loadPolyfill` requests inside the same hot workload are deduplicated or served from an in-runtime cache instead of crossing the bridge every time.

Pi SDK startup and Pi CLI startup show a meaningful reduction in `_loadPolyfill` call count and host bridge time versus the committed baseline, and the exact deltas are captured in committed benchmark artifacts.
The Hono startup/end-to-end control scenarios do not regress materially; if they do, the regression is explained in `scripts/ralph/progress.txt` and followed by a new story.

Append before/after benchmark deltas to `scripts/ralph/progress.txt` for the affected scenarios, including warm wall time, bridge calls per iteration, and the dominant IPC counters touched by this story.

If this story reveals another optimization opportunity or unresolved blocker, add it to `docs-internal/todo.md` and append a new `passes: false` follow-up story to `scripts/ralph/prd.json` before marking this story complete.
Relevant targeted tests pass.
Touched packages typecheck.
userStories.notes: Completed by caching bare-specifier polyfill probes plus package-internal resolution results inside read-only `node_modules` trees. The committed benchmark artifacts cut Pi startup `_loadPolyfill` wrapper counts roughly in half (`5240 -> 2523` for Pi SDK startup, `5247 -> 2528` for Pi CLI startup), while a direct sandbox probe now shows the remaining `_loadPolyfill` wrapper calls are unique and only `77` real non-`__bd:*` polyfill-body loads remain during Pi SDK startup. Aggregate `_loadPolyfill` method time in `comparison.md` is still confounded by unique `__bd:*` bridge-dispatch wrappers, so attribution follow-up `US-014` tracks that measurement gap explicitly.

userStories.title: Split `_loadPolyfill` benchmark attribution from `__bd:*` bridge dispatch
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need the module-load benchmark to distinguish real polyfill-body loads from generic `__bd:*` bridge-dispatch wrappers so future stories can tell whether `_loadPolyfill` regressions come from repeated polyfill misses or from other bridge work.
acceptanceCriteria: Committed benchmark artifacts break the current `_loadPolyfill` bucket into at least two signals: real polyfill-body loads and generic `__bd:*` bridge-dispatch wrappers.
Pi startup scenarios expose copy-ready before/after deltas for both signals so later progress entries do not need ad hoc in-sandbox probes to understand `_loadPolyfill` changes.
Focused regression coverage or summary tests prove the new attribution stays stable across repeated benchmark reruns.
Append before/after benchmark deltas to `scripts/ralph/progress.txt` for the affected scenarios, including warm wall time, bridge calls per iteration, and the new `_loadPolyfill` attribution fields.
Relevant targeted tests pass.
Touched packages typecheck.
userStories.notes: Completed by adding `_loadPolyfill` target attribution to IPC observability plus a benchmark-summary fallback classifier for older logs. The committed `summary.md` / `comparison.md` artifacts now expose copy-ready `real polyfill-body loads` vs `__bd:* bridge-dispatch wrappers` deltas for Pi startup scenarios.

userStories.title: Shrink `_loadPolyfill` payload transfer with identifier or hash caching
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need repeated polyfill payloads to travel once and then be referenced cheaply so Pi workloads stop paying multi-megabyte response costs.
acceptanceCriteria: Repeated polyfill source payloads are served by identifier, hash, manifest, or an equivalent cache-friendly protocol instead of sending the full body every time.
Pi SDK startup and Pi CLI startup show a meaningful reduction in BridgeResponse bytes and warm wall time versus baseline, and the committed artifacts make that byte reduction visible.
The new caching scheme remains correct across warm sessions, fresh sessions, and benchmark re-runs; there is regression coverage for cache hits and misses.

Append before/after benchmark deltas to `scripts/ralph/progress.txt` for the affected scenarios, including warm wall time, bridge calls per iteration, and the dominant IPC counters touched by this story.

If this story reveals another optimization opportunity or unresolved blocker, add it to `docs-internal/todo.md` and append a new `passes: false` follow-up story to `scripts/ralph/prd.json` before marking this story complete.
Relevant targeted tests pass.
Touched packages typecheck.
userStories.notes: Pi startup paths currently move roughly `11 MB` send / `0.9 MB` receive per iteration, with BridgeResponse traffic contributing `~9.36-9.72 MB` per iteration. `_loadPolyfill` payload reuse is the first byte-level target.

userStories.title: Stop resending large bridge bootstrap payloads on every execute
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need repeated executes to avoid retransmitting large static bootstrap payloads so the steady-state module-load path is not dominated by repeated setup bytes.
acceptanceCriteria: Repeated executions do not resend the same large bridge/bootstrap payload in full when the content is unchanged, or the benchmark proves a smaller equivalent design.
Committed benchmark artifacts expose before/after `Execute` frame size so the byte movement from this story is obvious.
Affected scenarios show a meaningful reduction in steady-state wall time or transport bytes versus baseline without breaking correctness.

Append before/after benchmark deltas to `scripts/ralph/progress.txt` for the affected scenarios, including warm wall time, bridge calls per iteration, and the dominant IPC counters touched by this story.

If this story reveals another optimization opportunity or unresolved blocker, add it to `docs-internal/todo.md` and append a new `passes: false` follow-up story to `scripts/ralph/prd.json` before marking this story complete.
Relevant targeted tests pass.
Touched packages typecheck.
userStories.notes: The current analysis shows an `Execute` frame of roughly `1.24 MB` per iteration. That makes repeated bootstrap transfer a plausible second-order cost even after `_loadPolyfill` call count drops.

userStories.title: Cache module resolution, package metadata, and filesystem probes
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need resolver and file existence checks on the hot startup path to be memoized so Pi workloads stop repeating the same expensive lookup work.
acceptanceCriteria: Positive and negative module resolution results, package metadata reads, and repeated filesystem probe results on the hot load path are memoized or otherwise deduplicated safely.
Pi startup scenarios show lower `_resolveModule` and `_fsExists` counts and host bridge time versus baseline, with exact before/after values captured in committed benchmark artifacts.
Regression coverage proves the new caching does not break module graph correctness, path canonicalization, or permission enforcement.

Append before/after benchmark deltas to `scripts/ralph/progress.txt` for the affected scenarios, including warm wall time, bridge calls per iteration, and the dominant IPC counters touched by this story.

If this story reveals another optimization opportunity or unresolved blocker, add it to `docs-internal/todo.md` and append a new `passes: false` follow-up story to `scripts/ralph/prd.json` before marking this story complete.
Relevant targeted tests pass.
Touched packages typecheck.
userStories.notes: Baseline secondary hotspots: `_resolveModule` is `34` calls and `~41-46 ms` per Pi iteration, while `_fsExists` rises to `38` calls and `~31.5 ms` in Pi SDK end-to-end and `44` calls with `~54.9 ms` in Pi CLI startup.

userStories.title: Preload or snapshot the hottest bootstrap assets
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need the hottest polyfills and bootstrap assets to arrive via snapshot or a preloaded bundle so the runtime does not reconstruct the same startup surface through bridge calls every run.
acceptanceCriteria: The hottest bootstrap assets are delivered through snapshot or another preloaded no-RPC path where it is actually cheaper than the current bridge-driven load behavior.
Committed benchmark artifacts compare the old and new bootstrap path, including any warm snapshot tradeoff, and record the effect on Pi startup/end-to-end scenarios.
The optimization keeps correctness for both simple Hono controls and Pi workloads, with focused regression coverage on whichever bootstrap surface changed.

Append before/after benchmark deltas to `scripts/ralph/progress.txt` for the affected scenarios, including warm wall time, bridge calls per iteration, and the dominant IPC counters touched by this story.

If this story reveals another optimization opportunity or unresolved blocker, add it to `docs-internal/todo.md` and append a new `passes: false` follow-up story to `scripts/ralph/prd.json` before marking this story complete.
Relevant targeted tests pass.
Touched packages typecheck.
userStories.notes: The current benchmark already pays a one-time `WarmSnapshot` transfer of about `1.05 MB` per scenario. This story should only land if a snapshot or preload path beats the current repeated bridge behavior in committed numbers.

userStories.title: Reduce the fixed per-session overhead
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need fresh-session startup to spend less time in session lifecycle bookkeeping so even low-RPC workloads like Hono are materially faster.
acceptanceCriteria: The fixed fresh-session overhead identified in the benchmark is reduced meaningfully from the current `~109 ms` baseline, or the story produces precise attribution that isolates the remaining dominant phase.
Hono startup and Hono end-to-end show measurable warm-wall improvement versus baseline, and any Pi gains or regressions are captured in committed benchmark artifacts.
Regression coverage protects the session lifecycle or runtime bookkeeping surfaces touched by this change.

Append before/after benchmark deltas to `scripts/ralph/progress.txt` for the affected scenarios, including warm wall time, bridge calls per iteration, and the dominant IPC counters touched by this story.

If this story reveals another optimization opportunity or unresolved blocker, add it to `docs-internal/todo.md` and append a new `passes: false` follow-up story to `scripts/ralph/prd.json` before marking this story complete.
Relevant targeted tests pass.
Touched packages typecheck.
userStories.notes: The fixed overhead shows up even in Hono (`~110 ms` out of `~188-202 ms` wall time) so it is likely session creation/destruction and adjacent bookkeeping rather than the module graph itself.

userStories.title: Expand the benchmark controls for cold, warm, and host comparisons
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need the module-load benchmark to cover true cold start, same-session replay, new-session replay, host control, and warm snapshot toggles so optimizations can be attributed to the right layer.
acceptanceCriteria: The committed benchmark suite includes true cold-start timing that covers `createNodeV8Runtime()`, same-session repeated import timing, new-session repeated import timing, and a host-Node control for the same scenarios.
The benchmark can compare warm snapshot enabled vs disabled for the tracked scenarios and commit the resulting artifact set.
The committed results format makes it clear which benchmark mode is being compared so future progress entries can distinguish startup, execute, snapshot, and control-path changes.

Append before/after benchmark deltas to `scripts/ralph/progress.txt` for the affected scenarios, including warm wall time, bridge calls per iteration, and the dominant IPC counters touched by this story.

If this story reveals another optimization opportunity or unresolved blocker, add it to `docs-internal/todo.md` and append a new `passes: false` follow-up story to `scripts/ralph/prd.json` before marking this story complete.
Relevant targeted tests pass.
Touched packages typecheck.
userStories.notes: The current benchmark excludes `createNodeV8Runtime()` from wall time and does not include a host control. Those missing controls make it too easy to confuse session cost, bootstrap cost, and actual steady-state module-load work.

userStories.title: Restore direct Pi CLI headless execution inside standalone NodeRuntime
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need standalone NodeRuntime headless Pi CLI execution to reach the provider path so the benchmark can drop the current CLI-module-graph plus SDK surrogate and measure the direct CLI path honestly.
acceptanceCriteria: A standalone `NodeRuntime.exec()` that imports or calls `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent/dist/main.js` or `dist/cli.js` in headless print mode reaches at least one mock Anthropic request and surfaces stdout inside the sandbox.
The `pi-cli-end-to-end` benchmark can switch from the current surrogate path back to the direct CLI headless path without regressing the committed artifact contract.
Focused regression coverage proves empty-stdin headless Pi CLI no longer exits `0` before provider traffic in standalone `NodeRuntime`.
Relevant targeted tests pass.
Touched packages typecheck.
userStories.notes: Fixed by switching the benchmark and regression harnesses to call `dist/main.js` directly inside standalone `NodeRuntime.exec()`, while forcing `process.stdin.isTTY = true` for explicit `--print` messages so Pi skips its piped-stdin probe and reaches the mock provider path.
